title: Potential impact of USTR's proposed fees
section_path: Potential impact of USTR's proposed fees
section_path_text: Potential impact of USTR's proposed fees
summary:
Main
By Nikolaos Tagoulis
The USTR Notice of Action issued on April 17 introducing fees up to $1.5 million on Chinese shipping companies and Chinese-built vessels has sparked industry-wide concerns about its potential impacts. While the fees are set to take effect 180 days from the announcement (on October 14), could still be amended following a public hearing scheduled by USTR on May 19. At first glance, the 180-day implementation buffer might seem procedural, but it could also embody a tactical move, in line with President Trump's negotiation methods: creating leverage through the credible threat of future action, while leaving space for reactions, renegotiations, or policy adjustments.
